perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials. Oral disclosures of confidential information shall not be subject to the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a conflict by stating oral disclosures are not subject to the agreement in Section 1, while Section 2 discusses the protection of orally disclosed information. This generates ambiguity as to whether oral disclosures are protected or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that oral disclosures are not subject to the terms of the agreement. However, the contradicted text outlines obligations for the receiving party regarding confidential information that has been disclosed, which implicitly includes orally disclosed information, creating a direct conflict. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, except that the receiving party shall have a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use any ideas or concepts derived from such confidential information for internal purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the exception regarding a perpetual, non-exclusive license in Section 3, it contradicts the initial statement that the agreement doesn't grant any rights to the receiving party. This undermines the original intent of the agreement to protect ownership of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, except that the receiving party shall have a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use any ideas or concepts derived from such confidential information for internal purposes. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that nothing in the agreement grants rights to the other party regarding confidential information. The changed text introduces an exception, granting the receiving party a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use ideas derived from confidential information. This directly contradicts the original statement by granting a specific right, thus creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information, except where such Confidential Information is required for ongoing internal projects. In those cases, destruction or return is not required.
perturbation.explanation: By adding an exception for 'ongoing internal projects' in Section 3, this contradicts the original obligation to return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information. This creates uncertainty as to when the recipient is actually required to return or destroy information and undermines the disclosing party's control over its confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information, except where such Confidential Information is required for ongoing internal projects. In those cases, destruction or return is not required. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the recipient's obligation to return or destroy confidential information, allowing them to retain it for ongoing internal projects. This directly contradicts the original obligation to return or destroy 'all copies,' creating an inconsistency as to when the recipient is actually required to return or destroy information. This inconsistency undermines the disclosing party's control over its confidential information, as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement, however the disclosing Party shall only be entitled to receive monetary damages, not injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: The modification restricts remedies to monetary damages only, negating access to injunctive relief. This creates a contradiction within the text and limits the protection afforded to the disclosing party in the event of a breach in Section 3, where it initially stated they were entitled to injunctive relief. This limits legal protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement, however the disclosing Party shall only be entitled to receive monetary damages, not injunctive relief. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the disclosing party is 'only be entitled to receive monetary damages, not injunctive relief,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text's statement that 'the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief.' This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the remedies available to the disclosing party in case of a breach.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date, at which point either party can automatically renew for another three-year term unless notification is given by both parties to end the agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct contradiction by stating that the requirement to protect confidential information does not survive termination of the agreement in Section 9, even though the original text says it does. Additionally, stating it could renew automatically causes uncertainty in the original terms where it would only stay in effect for 3 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date, at which point either party can automatically renew for another three-year term unless notification is given by both parties to end the agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two key contradictions. First, it states that the agreement automatically renews unless both parties give notice, contradicting the original agreement's duration of three years. Second, it stipulates that the confidentiality obligations do not survive termination, directly conflicting with the original provision that they do. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, since the changed text and contradicted text are the same.
